one_line: Queen Velu Nachiyar of Sivaganga and her commander Kuyili, leaders of late-18th-century anti-colonial resistance in Tamil Nadu.

appearance.age_look: Velu Nachiyar appears in her early 30s with a dignified, athletic build; Kuyili appears late 20s with a compact, muscular frame.
appearance.face: Both have oval faces with warm brown complexions; Velu has sharp, commanding eyes and a calm, resolute default expression, while Kuyili has slightly narrower eyes with a fierce, focused default expression and a faint old scar across her left cheek.
appearance.hair: Velu’s long black hair is oiled and braided into a single thick plait wrapped with a narrow red cloth band and jasmine flowers, middle-parted at the front, with a simple thin gold maang tikka; Kuyili’s black hair is tied into a tight, practical low bun wrapped in a dark cloth strip with no ornaments.
appearance.costume: Velu Nachiyar wears a late-18th-century Tamil aristocratic war attire: a deep maroon silk sari with a narrow gold border, worn in a practical madisar-style drape over a fitted dark green blouse with elbow-length sleeves, pleats hitched slightly above the ankle for mobility; over this she has a sleeveless, waist-length quilted cotton armour vest in muted indigo with simple vertical stitching, tied with cloth cords at the sides, and a plain cream angavastram scarf folded and slung across her left shoulder; she wears minimal jewellery—small round gold earrings, a thin gold nose ring, a single medium-width gold bangle on each wrist and a black-bead mangalsutra-like chain—plus simple brown leather sandals; Kuyili wears a coarse handloom cotton outfit in earthy tones: a dull brown knee-length half-sari-like wrap over a faded off-white fitted choli with short sleeves, and a dark olive cotton waistcloth tied tightly with visible knots; she has a plain dark cotton dupatta wrapped diagonally across her torso like a bandolier, bare feet wrapped at the instep with narrow cloth strips for grip, and no metal jewellery, only a simple black thread around her neck.
appearance.signature: Velu carries a sheathed curved steel tulwar-style sword with a brass hilt and a plain brown leather scabbard hanging from a dark leather belt, while Kuyili carries a long clay oil-pot with a rope harness slung on her back and a small flint fire-striker at her waist, referencing her role in the popular memory of the armoury attack.

ref_desc: Velu Nachiyar is a warm-brown, sharp-eyed queen in a deep maroon madisar sari over a dark green blouse with an indigo quilted armour vest, jasmine-wrapped braid, minimal gold jewellery and a curved tulwar, beside Kuyili, a scarred, fierce warm-brown soldier in dull brown and off-white cotton wraps with a tight low bun, cloth-bound feet, and a rope-slung clay oil-pot and flint striker, all in maroon, indigo, dark green, earthy brown and cream tones.
sheet_prompt: Draw a full-body anime-style model sheet of two fixed characters from late-18th-century Sivaganga, Tamil Nadu: Velu Nachiyar, a dignified warm-brown woman in her early 30s with an oval face and sharp eyes, long oiled black hair middle-parted and braided into a thick plait wrapped with a red cloth band and jasmine flowers, wearing a deep maroon silk sari with a narrow gold border draped madisar-style over a fitted dark green blouse with elbow-length sleeves, the sari pleats hitched slightly above the ankle, a sleeveless waist-length indigo quilted cotton armour vest tied at the sides, a folded cream angavastram across her left shoulder, small round gold earrings, thin gold nose ring, single gold bangles, a simple black-bead chain, brown leather sandals, and at her hip a curved steel tulwar with brass hilt in a plain brown leather scabbard on a dark leather belt; beside her Kuyili, a compact, muscular warm-brown woman in her late 20s with an oval face, narrower fierce eyes, a faint scar across her left cheek, and black hair pulled into a tight low bun wrapped with a dark cloth strip, wearing a coarse dull brown knee-length wrap like a half-sari over a faded off-white fitted short-sleeve choli, a dark olive cotton waistcloth tied tightly with visible knots, and a plain dark cotton dupatta slung diagonally across her torso like a bandolier, bare feet wrapped at the instep with narrow cloth strips, no jewellery except a simple black thread at her neck; she carries on her back a long clay oil-pot held by a rope harness and at her waist a small flint fire-striker; show them from multiple angles with clear views of face, hair, sari drape, armour vest, wrapped garments, weapon, oil-pot harness and colour scheme of maroon, indigo, dark green, earthy brown and cream.
